items.question: What does an uncodified constitution rely on?
items.correctAnswer: a mix of statutes, court decisions, and conventions instead of one single text
incorrectAnswers: only royal decrees with no parliament
rules that cannot be written down
items.explanation: Some countries build constitutional practice from several sources over time.

items.question: What is a bill of rights or rights chapter in many constitutions meant to do?
items.correctAnswer: list protected freedoms and limits on government power
incorrectAnswers: set tax brackets for one year only
name every street in the country
items.explanation: Rights provisions guard individuals and groups from abuse of state power.

items.question: What is a written constitution in comparative terms?
items.correctAnswer: a single formal document (or set) that sets up government structure and powers
incorrectAnswers: only a collection of folk tales
tax rates updated every week by one clerk
rules that apply only to foreign visitors
items.explanation: Codified constitutions organize branches, rights, and amendment rules in one place.

items.question: What is constitutional supremacy?
items.correctAnswer: ordinary laws and government actions must follow the higher rules of the constitution
incorrectAnswers: local sports leagues override all courts
only treaties from one decade apply
the constitution applies only to diplomats
items.explanation: When constitutions are supreme, conflicting statutes can be struck down or reformed.

items.question: Why do constitutions often need special amendment procedures?
items.correctAnswer: so basic rules cannot be changed as easily as ordinary day-to-day laws
incorrectAnswers: to ban all future court review
to require daily public votes on every line
items.explanation: Supermajorities or referendums protect core arrangements from momentary majorities.

items.question: Why do federal constitutions divide powers between levels of government?
items.correctAnswer: to balance national unity with regional self-rule and prevent over-centralization
items.explanation: Layered authority lets local and national governments each operate within defined spheres.
